description: Letter from Abū Manṣūr to his 'father' (it seems father-in-law) al-Rayyis Hibatallāh the judge, representative of the Nagid David, in Alexandria: Dating: Based on the name of the Nagid, either 1237–1300 (if David I) or 1355–1410 (if David II). In Arabic script with occasional Hebrew script (with multiple errors) mixed in. Goitein refers to the script as "childish," in part because it is relatively easy to read and has diacritics. The letter is a detailed business letter concerning the textile trade. See Goitein's note card for some of the content.
description_he: מכתב בערבית לאלכסנדריה. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
